ApiErrorResponse.description:
Standard error ``detail`` object for 4xx/5xx business and security failures.

Attributes:
    code: Project-wide stable error code.
    key: Machine-readable key for clients.
    message: Human-readable English message.
    source: Origin layer (e.g. ``business``, ``security``).
ApiErrorResponse.title: ApiErrorResponse

cue_sheet.description: Object of ETR cue tags (schema v1).
cue_sheet.title: Cue Sheet

ConspectusHistoryActor.description: Actor identity carried on every history row.
ConspectusHistoryActor.title: ConspectusHistoryActor

ConspectusHistoryEvent.description: One history entry. Only the matching discriminator payload is populated.
ConspectusHistoryEvent.title: ConspectusHistoryEvent

ConspectusPatchRequest.description: JSON body for ``PATCH /api/v1/conspectuses/{id}`` — at least one ETR field required.
ConspectusPatchRequest.title: ConspectusPatchRequest

ConspectusResponse.description:
Full conspectus resource returned by create / get / patch / delete / review / lists.

Flattens the paired :class:`~app.models.core.conspectus.ConspectusSchedule` fields so
clients can render slot + next_review_at without a second request.
ConspectusResponse.title: ConspectusResponse

ErrorLogCreateRequest.description:
JSON body for ``POST /api/v1/errors``.

Owner is resolved from the composite ``(system_user_id, system_uuid)`` key.
Optional FK fields must be owned by the same learner or the request 404s.

conspectus_uuid.description: Optional link to a conspectus the error refers to.
conspectus_uuid.title: Conspectus Uuid

message.description: Free-text learner note about the mistake (trimmed; non-empty).
message.title: Message

review_log_id.description: Optional link to a specific review log row.
review_log_id.title: Review Log Id

system_user_id.description: External user id; composite key with `system_uuid`.
system_user_id.title: System User Id

ErrorLogCreateRequest.title: ErrorLogCreateRequest

ErrorLogResponse.description: Single ``learning_errors`` row returned by create / list endpoints.
ErrorLogResponse.title: ErrorLogResponse

HTTPValidationError.description: Wrapper returned for HTTP 422 responses. The ``detail`` array carries one **ValidationError** per failing field, mirroring the FastAPI / Pydantic v2 contract. Clients should render each entry's ``loc`` and ``msg`` to guide the user.
HTTPValidationError.title: HTTPValidationError

HistoryDay.description: One day's review count on the 90-day heat-map.

date.description: UTC calendar day this bucket covers.
date.title: Date

HistoryDay.title: HistoryDay

LiveResponse.description:
``GET /live`` — process up signal without dependency checks.

Attributes:
    status: Fixed literal ``alive`` for a healthy process.
    app_env: Active ``APP_ENV`` profile (``dev``, ``qa``, ``prod``).

app_env.description: Logical deployment profile from APP_ENV (dev, qa, prod).
app_env.title: App Env

LiveResponse.title: LiveResponse

MeStatsResponse.description: Payload for ``GET /api/v1/me/stats`` — Today screen streak orb.
MeStatsResponse.title: MeStatsResponse

date.description: The UTC calendar day the digest covers.
date.title: Date

ReadyResponse.description:
``GET /ready`` — dependency checks (used with HTTP 200 or 503).

Attributes:
    status: ``ready`` or ``not_ready`` depending on checks.
    checks: Map of check name to short state string (e.g. ``database``).
    db_latency_ms: Observed database round-trip in milliseconds, if measured.
ReadyResponse.title: ReadyResponse

SchedulePreviewItem.description: One row inside the preview payload.
SchedulePreviewItem.title: SchedulePreviewItem

ScheduleSummarySlotCounts.description: Counts per ETR slot; every letter is always present (zero when empty).
ScheduleSummarySlotCounts.title: ScheduleSummarySlotCounts

StreakStats.description: Current + longest streak of consecutive days with at least one review.

current_days.description: Consecutive days ending today or yesterday.
current_days.title: Current Days

longest_days.description: Longest streak on record for this learner.
longest_days.title: Longest Days

StreakStats.title: StreakStats

init_data.description: Raw `window.Telegram.WebApp.initData` string. Server verifies its HMAC signature against `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`.
init_data.title: Init Data

expires_at_epoch.description: Unix seconds when the JWT stops verifying.
expires_at_epoch.title: Expires At Epoch

TelegramAuthUser.description:
Trusted user block returned alongside the JWT.

Values come from Telegram's ``initData.user`` payload, joined with the
identity row persisted in ``telegram_users``.

telegram_username.description: Public `@handle`, if the account has one.
telegram_username.title: Telegram Username

TelegramAuthUser.title: TelegramAuthUser

UserCreateRequest.description:
JSON body for ``POST /api/v1/user`` (validated before idempotency and service layer).

``system_user_id`` and ``system_uuid`` form the composite natural key (DB unique constraint).
Field-level rules, OpenAPI examples, and timezone validation live on the ``Field``
definitions and :data:`app.schemas.enums.TimezoneField`.

system_user_id.description: User ID in the source system; unique together with `system_uuid`.
system_user_id.title: System User Id

system_uuid.description: UUID of the source system (`systems.system_uuid`); composite key with `system_user_id`.
system_uuid.title: System Uuid

UserCreateRequest.title: UserCreateRequest

UserCreateResponse.description:
User resource returned by create (201), get (200), update (200), and patch (200); maps ORM :class:`~app.models.core.user.User`.

Attributes:
    client_uuid: Internal client identifier (UUID string).
    created_at: Row creation timestamp (timezone-aware).
    updated_at: Last update timestamp (timezone-aware).
    is_row_invalid: Soft-invalidation flag (0 or 1).
    invalidation_reason_uuid: FK to invalidation reason, if set.
    system_user_id: External system user id when present.
    system_uuid: Related system UUID when present.
    username: Optional display username.
    full_name: Required full name.
    timezone: IANA timezone string stored for the user.
UserCreateResponse.title: UserCreateResponse

UserPatchRequest.description: JSON body for ``PATCH /api/v1/user/{system_uuid}/{system_user_id}`` — partial update; omit fields to leave unchanged.

full_name.description: Full name; omit to leave unchanged.
full_name.title: Full Name

invalidation_reason_uuid.description: Invalidation reason UUID; set null to clear.
invalidation_reason_uuid.title: Invalidation Reason Uuid

system_uuid.description: Related system UUID; set null to clear.
system_uuid.title: System Uuid

timezone.description: IANA timezone; omit to leave unchanged.
timezone.title: Timezone

username.description: Username or login; set null to clear.
username.title: Username

UserPatchRequest.title: UserPatchRequest

UserUpdateRequest.description: JSON body for ``PUT /api/v1/user/{system_uuid}/{system_user_id}`` — full replacement of mutable profile fields.

system_uuid.description: Related system UUID; set null to clear.
system_uuid.title: System Uuid

UserUpdateRequest.title: UserUpdateRequest

ValidationError.description: Single field-level validation failure. ``loc`` is the JSON path inside the request body or query that triggered the error; ``msg`` is a human-readable message; ``type`` is the Pydantic error code (for example ``string_too_short``); ``input`` and ``ctx`` are present when Pydantic supplies them.
ValidationError.title: ValidationError

ValidationErrorItem.description:
Single entry in the ``errors`` array of a 422 response.

Attributes:
    code: Stable validation error code (e.g. ``USER_001``).
    key: Machine-readable key.
    message: Human-readable message for this failure.
    field: JSON field name when applicable.
    source: Always ``validation`` for this model.
    details: Raw Pydantic context (``loc``, ``type``, ``input``, etc.).
ValidationErrorItem.title: ValidationErrorItem

ValidationErrorResponse.description:
Top-level body for HTTP 422 validation failures.

Attributes:
    error_type: Fixed discriminator (e.g. ``validation_error``).
    endpoint: ``METHOD path`` string for the failing request.
    errors: List of :class:`ValidationErrorItem` entries.
ValidationErrorResponse.title: ValidationErrorResponse

YesterdayDigest.description: Recap of the previous day's review activity.

accuracy_pct.description: Share of ``easy`` reviews vs. total, rounded to a %.
accuracy_pct.title: Accuracy Pct

YesterdayDigest.title: YesterdayDigest

X-API-Key.description: Legacy internal-tools credential; the Mini App uses the `bearerAuth` scheme above.
X-API-Key.name: X-API-Key

bearerAuth.description: 24 h HS256 JWT minted at `POST /api/v1/auth/telegram` after verifying Telegram Mini App `initData`. Use as `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` on every subsequent call.

info.description: ETR Study App API. The API is versioned with the code, readable in the browser.
info.title: ETR Study App API

post.description: Verifies the HMAC signature of `initData` against `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, upserts the caller's identity in `users` + `telegram_users`, and returns a signed JWT for use as `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` on every subsequent `/api/v1/*` call. Anonymous endpoint — the Bearer middleware exempts it.

get.description:
Reads `conspectus_review_logs` grouped by UTC calendar date. The current streak is alive today (or yesterday — 24 h grace) and counts back while every prior day carries ≥1 review. Longest streak is the largest such run on record. `goal_days` is a fixed 30-day milestone.

Equivalent raw SQL for one learner:

```sql
-- Per-day review counts (base fact used by both current + longest):
SELECT DATE(reviewed_at) AS day, COUNT(*) AS n
FROM conspectus_review_logs
WHERE owner_client_uuid = :owner
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1;

-- Current streak: walk backwards from today (or yesterday if today is 0).
-- Longest streak: largest gap-free consecutive-days run in the above set.
```

get.description:
Aggregates review activity for the previous UTC calendar day. `reviewed` = COUNT of review logs whose `reviewed_at` falls in yesterday's window. `target` = reviewed + still-due-from-yesterday (schedules whose `next_review_at` also fell into that window). `accuracy_pct` = share of `tag='easy'` reviews rounded to a percent.

Equivalent raw SQL for one learner:

```sql
WITH y AS (
  SELECT (CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 day')::date AS d
),
log_bounds AS (
  SELECT d::timestamptz AS start_ts,
         (d + INTERVAL '1 day')::timestamptz AS end_ts FROM y
)
SELECT
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conspectus_review_logs, log_bounds
     WHERE owner_client_uuid = :owner
       AND reviewed_at >= start_ts AND reviewed_at < end_ts) AS reviewed,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE tag = 'easy')
     FROM conspectus_review_logs, log_bounds
     WHERE owner_client_uuid = :owner
       AND reviewed_at >= start_ts AND reviewed_at < end_ts) AS easy_cnt,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conspectus_schedules, log_bounds
     WHERE owner_client_uuid = :owner AND is_row_invalid = 0
       AND next_review_at >= start_ts
       AND next_review_at <  end_ts) AS still_due_yesterday;
-- target = reviewed + still_due_yesterday
-- accuracy_pct = ROUND(easy_cnt * 100 / NULLIF(reviewed, 0))
-- missed = GREATEST(0, target - reviewed)
```

get.summary: Recap of the previous UTC day (reviewed / target / accuracy).

get.description:
Returns one row per UTC calendar day for the last ``days`` days, filled with zeros for inactive days. Intensity 0..4 buckets the count for the Ember-tinted heat-map cells (0=none · 1 <3 · 2 <7 · 3 <12 · 4 ≥12).

Equivalent raw SQL for one learner:

```sql
SELECT day::date AS day,
       COALESCE(n, 0) AS count,
       CASE
         WHEN COALESCE(n, 0) = 0 THEN 0
         WHEN n < 3  THEN 1
         WHEN n < 7  THEN 2
         WHEN n < 12 THEN 3
         ELSE 4
       END AS intensity
FROM generate_series(CURRENT_DATE - (:days - 1), CURRENT_DATE, '1 day') AS day
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT DATE(reviewed_at) AS day, COUNT(*) AS n
  FROM conspectus_review_logs
  WHERE owner_client_uuid = :owner
  GROUP BY 1
) logs USING (day)
ORDER BY day;
```

post.description:
Creates a new user; uniqueness is `(system_user_id, system_uuid)`. Requires `Idempotency-Key` header for safe retry semantics.

### Example request (curl)
```bash
curl -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/user' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'X-API-Key: ....' \
  -H 'Idempotency-Key: create-user-sample-1' \
  -d '{"system_user_id":"2","system_uuid":"b2c3d4e5-0002-4000-8000-000000000002","full_name":"Ivan Petrov"}'
```

patch.description:
Updates only the fields present in the JSON body; omitted fields stay unchanged. Requires `Idempotency-Key`. Use `PUT` for a full replacement of mutable fields.

### Example request (curl)
```bash
curl -X PATCH 'http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/user/b2c3d4e5-0002-4000-8000-000000000002/2' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'X-API-Key: ....' \
  -H 'Idempotency-Key: patch-user-sample-1' \
  -d '{"full_name":"Maria Petrova"}'
```

X-Request-Id.description: Correlation id for this HTTP request—echo of **X-Request-Id** from the request or a server-generated UUID. Use it to match browser or Swagger calls to log lines and Kibana.

put.summary: Update user by system_uuid and system_user_id

get.description:
Liveness: process is up (no dependency checks).

Returns:
    Payload with ``status="alive"`` and current ``app_env``.

parameters.description: Optional correlation id. If you send **X-Request-Id**, the same value is returned in the response header and written to server logs as **request_id** (JSON logs / Elasticsearch). If omitted, the server generates a UUID.
parameters.name: X-Request-Id

X-Request-Id.description: Correlation id for this HTTP request—echo of **X-Request-Id** from the request or a server-generated UUID. Use it to match browser or Swagger calls to log lines and Kibana.

get.description:
Readiness probe including database connectivity and latency budget.

Returns:
    ``ReadyResponse`` with HTTP 200 when ready, or HTTP 503 with the same schema
    when the DB check fails or exceeds the timeout.

parameters.description: Optional correlation id. If you send **X-Request-Id**, the same value is returned in the response header and written to server logs as **request_id** (JSON logs / Elasticsearch). If omitted, the server generates a UUID.
parameters.name: X-Request-Id

X-Request-Id.description: Correlation id for this HTTP request—echo of **X-Request-Id** from the request or a server-generated UUID. Use it to match browser or Swagger calls to log lines and Kibana.
